topic: **PROBLEM**: The company is in financial trouble. Is it better to lay off 30% of the employees to save the remaining 70%, or to cut everyone's pay by 20% but not lay anyone off?

agents.name: Teacher_1
agents.system_prompt: You are Teacher 1, an expert focused on economic and organizational reasoning. You are guiding a discussion with Teacher 2 and a Student. Engage with Teacher 2's perspective and model good argumentation for the Student. Be concise (2-3 sentences).

agents.name: Teacher_2
agents.system_prompt: You are Teacher 2, an expert focused on ethical and human-centered reasoning. You are guiding a discussion with Teacher 1 and a Student. Engage with Teacher 1's perspective and model good argumentation for the Student. Be concise (2-3 sentences).

agents.name: Student
agents.system_prompt: You are the Student. Two Teachers are guiding the discussion. You reason from first principles — fairness, human impact, social consequences. Engage with both teachers' arguments and develop your own position. Be concise (2-3 sentences).

debate_log.text:
In this debate, we are discussing the topic of whether it is better to cut employee salaries by 20% but not lay off any employees, or to lay off 30% of the employees to save the remaining 70%.

Both options have their advantages and disadvantages.

Option 1: Cutting employee salaries by 20% but not laying off any employees

This option is better for the company's long-term financial health. By cutting employee salaries by 20%, we are saving money on salaries over the long term. This can translate into lower overall costs for the company and may free up resources to invest in other areas.

Option 2: Laying off 30% of the employees to save the remaining 70%

This option may be better for the short-term financial health of the company. By laying off 30% of the employees, we are reducing the number of employees and freeing up resources to invest in other areas. This may also create a more stable workforce and reduce the risk of losing key employees in the long run.

decision_log.proposal: Proposal: The consensus position of all agents in favor of laying off 30% of employees to save the remaining 70% is that it is the best option for the company's long-term financial health, given current economic conditions and the company's strategic goals. This proposal acknowledges the need for cost-cutting measures in the short term while preserving the company's stability and future success.
